Correct Answer: Both A and B
Show explanation
Explanation

The smallest Jamarat is called Jamarat-ul-Sughra and Jamarat-ul-Oola

277

Three upright stones are called?

Correct Answer: Jamarat
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The three upright stones are called "Jamarat".
  • It stoned by pilgrims during the ritual in Mina.
278

When is the stoning of Jamarat performed during Hajj?

Correct Answer: Last days of Hajj
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The stoning of Jamarat is performed during Hajj on the days of Eid al-Adha, specifically on the 10th, 11th, and 12th days of Zul-Hajj.
279

What does the term Jamarat refer to in Hajj?

Correct Answer: Stoning pillars in Mina
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Jamarat in Hajj involves stoning three pillars in Mina.
  • It symbolizing the rejection of temptations.
  • It commemorating Prophet Ibrahim's defiance against Satan.
280

What is most important step after Waquf?

Correct Answer: Tawaf
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Tawaf involves circumambulating the Kaaba seven times in a counterclockwise direction.
  • It is symbolizing unity, submission.
  • It is external nature of worship in Islam.
281

How many Meeqats of Kaaba?

Correct Answer: 5
Show explanation
Explanation

The five miqats for pilgrims performing Hajj or Umrah are; 

  • 1. Dhul Hulayfa
  • 2. Al-Juhfa
  • 3. Qarn al-Manazil
  • 4. Yalamlam
  • 5. Dhat Irq
282

From Mina, Muzdalfa is______miles away.

Correct Answer: 3
Show explanation
Explanation
  • From Mina, Muzdalifa is a location located 3 miles away from Makkah in Saudi Arabia.
283

Muzdalfa is located_____miles away from Makkah.

Correct Answer: 6
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Muzdalifa is a location located 6 miles away from Makkah in Saudi Arabia.
284

How many undesirable acts of Ihram are there?

Correct Answer: 6
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The six undesirable acts of Ihram, also known as the prohibitions, include:
  • 1. Clipping the nails
  • 2. Removing hair
  • 3. Wearing perfume
  • 4. Wearing stitched clothing
  • 5. Hunting
  • 6. Engaging in marital relations
285

How many permitted acts of Ihram are there?

Correct Answer: 4
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The four permitted acts of Ihram are:
  • 1. Waring the prescribed clothing: Pilgrims wear two pieces of white, seamless cloth.
  • 2. Talbiyah: Reciting the specific supplication expressing the intention for Hajj or Umrah.
  • 3. Trimming or shaving the hair: Pilgrims typically trim or shave their hair as a symbolic act of humility.
  • 4. Certain restrictions: Avoiding specific actions, such as cutting nails, using scented products, or engaging in marital relations during the pilgrimage.
286

Muzdalfa is a?

Correct Answer: Plain
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Muzdalifah is located between Arafat and Mina.
  • Pilgrims spend the night here on the 9th Zul-Hijjah after leaving Arafat.
  • They collect pebbles for the ritual of Stoning the Devil at Mina.
  • Maghrib and Isha prayers are performed at Muzdalifah.
  • After Muzdalifah, pilgrims proceed to Mina for the Stoning of the Devil.
287

On Hajj day (9th Zul-Hajj) Imam leads_____prayers at Arafat which are offered jointly pilgrims.

Correct Answer: Zuhr and Asar
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Arafat is a plain near Mecca.
  • Pilgrims stand in prayerful reflection during the Wuquf at Arafat on the 9th day of Dhu al-Hijjah.
  • It is a day of repentance and seeking forgiveness.
  • Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) delivered his Farewell Sermon at Arafat.
  • Pilgrims gather here (Arafat) to fulfill a major Hajj ritual before moving to Muzdalifah
288

How many Khutbas at Masjid-e-Nimra are recited during Hajj?

Correct Answer: 1
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Masjid-e-Nimra is a mosque near Arafat in Mecca.
  • Pilgrims gather at Masjid-e-Nimra on the 9th day of Dhu al-Hijjah.
  • It is also known as the "Musalla".
  • The Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) delivered his Farewell Sermon at this location.
  • Pilgrims perform Dhuhr and Asr prayers combined at Masjid-e-Nimra during Hajj.
289

What is the meaning of Hijr-e-Aswad ?

Correct Answer: Black Stone
Show explanation
Explanation

What is the meaning of Hajr-e-Aswad?

Black Stone

290

How many Khutba are recited during Hajj?

Correct Answer: 1
Show explanation
Explanation

One khutba is recited during Hajj. Al-Imran is the surah in which Hajj is commanded. Holy Prophet (PBUH) sacrificed 63 camels during hajj. Adam and Hazrat Hawa performed the first ever Hajj.

291

Where do the Pilgrims go from Arafat?

Correct Answer: Muzdalfa
Show explanation
Explanation

After spending the day at Arafat during the Hajj pilgrimage, pilgrims proceed to Muzdalifah. This symbolic journey is an integral part of the Hajj rituals, following the traditions of Prophet Muhammad SAW.

292

From where does Sayee commence?

Correct Answer: Safaa
Show explanation
Explanation

Sayee, which is the act of walking seven times back and forth between the hills of Safa and Marwah, begins at Safa and concludes at Marwah. Pilgrims start at Safa and complete the seven rounds, finishing at Marwah during the Hajj pilgrimage.

293

How many rounds are paid between Safaa and Marwa during Hajj?

Correct Answer: 7
Show explanation
Explanation

During the Hajj pilgrimage, pilgrims perform seven rounds of Tawaf around the Kaaba. Tawaf is the act of walking around the Kaaba in a counterclockwise direction, and it is an integral part of the pilgrimage rituals.

294

Rami is held during Hajj at?

Correct Answer: Mina
Show explanation
Explanation
Rami al-Jamarat sometimes referred to as the “Stoning of the Devil” is a rite carried out by Hajj pilgrims whereby small pebbles are thrown at three stone structures in Mina. The act of throwing stones at the Jamarat is known as “Rami”.
295

After performing Sayee Hujjaj go to?

Correct Answer: Mina
Show explanation
Explanation
  • After performing Sayee, Hujjaj (pilgrims) typically go to Mina.
  • Mina is one of the locations where certain rituals of Hajj are performed, and it is visited after completing the Sa'i between Safa and Marwah.
296

What is Yum-ul-Tarvia?

Correct Answer: Staying at Mina After Isha on 8th Zul-Hajj
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Yawm al-Tarwiyah," it is the 8th day of Dhu al-Hijjah.
  • Pilgrims in Mecca enter into a state of ihram on this day, making preparations for the upcoming Hajj rites.
297

Which Kalimah is recited during Hajj on way to Mina?

Correct Answer: Kalimah-e-Tauheed
Show explanation
Explanation

  •  Kalma e Touheed is recited during Hajj on the way to Mina.

    لَآ اِلٰهَ اِلَّا اللهُ وَحْدَهٗ لَا شَرِيْكَ لَهٗ لَهُ الْمُلْكُ وَ لَهُ الْحَمْدُ يُحْىٖ وَ يُمِيْتُ وَ هُوَحَیٌّ لَّا يَمُوْتُ اَبَدًا اَبَدًاؕ ذُو الْجَلَالِ وَالْاِكْرَامِؕ بِيَدِهِ الْخَيْرُؕ وَهُوَ عَلٰى كُلِّ شیْ ٍٔ قَدِیْرٌؕ

298

Which stone of Kaaba wall is kissed?

Correct Answer: Hijr-e-Aswad
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The stone of the Kaaba wall that is kissed is called "Hijr-e-Aswad."
  • Pilgrims often seek to touch or kiss the Black Stone (Hijr-e-Aswad) during the Tawaf.
299

After completing the seven rounds of Kaaba where do the Hajji go?

Correct Answer: Sayee
Show explanation
Explanation
  • After completing the seven rounds (Tawaf) around the Kaaba, Hajj pilgrims proceed to perform the Sa'i, walking seven times back and forth between the hills of Safa and Marwah.
300

The fourth circle around Kaaba is?

Correct Answer: Mowaqeet
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first and innermost circle around Kaaba is? Masjid-e-Haram
  • The second circle around Kaaba is? Makkah Mukaramah
  • The third circle around Kaba is? Haram
  • The fourth circle around Kaba is? Mowaqeet
301

The third circle around Kaaba is?

Correct Answer: Haram
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first and innermost circle around Kaaba is? Masjid-e-Haram
  • The second circle around Kaaba is? Makkah Mukaramah
  • The third circle around Kaba is? Haram
  • The fourth circle around Kaba is? Mowaqeet
302

The second circle around Kaaba is?

Correct Answer: Makkah Mukaramah
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first and innermost circle around Kaaba is? Masjid-e-Haram
  • The second circle around Kaaba is? Makkah Mukaramah
  • The third circle around Kaba is? Haram
  • The fourth circle around Kaba is? Mowaqeet
303

The first and foremost Farz of Hajj and Umrah is?

Correct Answer: Ihram
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The first and innermost circle around Kaaba is? Masjid-e-Haram
  • The second circle around Kaaba is? Makkah Mukaramah
  • The third circle around Kaba is? Haram
  • The fourth circle around Kaba is? Mowaqeet
304

At____, Hujjaj assume the state of Ihram.

Correct Answer: Meekat
Show explanation
Explanation
  • At Meekat, Hujjaj (pilgrims) assume the state of Ihram.
  • Meekat is a designated boundary point outside of which pilgrims must enter into the sacred state of Ihram before proceeding to perform Hajj or Umrah.
305

The place where no one can enter without putting on Ihram is?

Correct Answer: Mowaqeet
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The place where no one can enter without putting on Ihram is Mowaqeet.
306

What to read while entering Haram Sharif during Hajj in Ahram?

Correct Answer: Talbia (Talbiyah)
Show explanation
Explanation

Bismillahi wal hamdulillahi wassaulatu a'laa rausulillah, Allauhummaghfirlee zunoobee waf tahlee abwaaba rauhmatik.

307

Prohibitions and restrictions of Ihram are?

Correct Answer: 8
Show explanation
Explanation
  • 1. Cutting Hair or Nails
  • 2. Wearing Scented Oils
  • 3. Marital Relations
  • 4. Hunting
  • 5. Wearing Stitched Clothing
  • 6. Swearing or Arguing
  • 7. Covering the Head for Men
  • 8. Removing or Killing Plants
308

A person who is hindered after the intended for Hajj or Umrah is called?

Correct Answer: Muhsar
Show explanation
Explanation
  • A person who is hindered after intending for Hajj or Umrah is called Muhsar.
309

Surah Hajj consist of____Rukus and 78 Ayats.

Correct Answer: 10
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Surah Hajj consists of 10 Rukus and 78 Ayats.
310

What is the number of Hajj among pillers?

Correct Answer: 5
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Five Pillars of Islam:
  • 1: Shahada (confession of faith)
  • 2: Salat (The Prayer)
  • 3: Zakat (Compulsory Charity)
  • 4: Sawm (fasting, especially during the month of Ramadan)
  • 5: Hajj (the pilgrimage to Mecca)
311

A person between 90 and 99 years old

Correct Answer: Nonagenarian
Show explanation
Explanation

A person having age between 90 and 99 years old is called Nonagenarian.

 Nonagenarian means نوے سال کی عمر کا آدمی


312

Who is the only Sahaba without seeing the Holy Prophet (PBUH)?

Correct Answer: Awais Qarni (RA)
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Awais Karni (RA) in the history of Islam only sahabi without seeing Holy Prophet (ﷺ).

..

Awais Karni was born in Asir region of Saudi Arabia in 594CE.

He was 28 years of age when the Holy Prophet (ﷺ) migrated to Madinah۔

313

The synonym of the word 'Gratify' is?

Correct Answer: Satisfy
Show explanation
Explanation

Gratify means خوش اور اطمینان بخشنا ہونا اطمینان دلانا

Satisfy means اطمینان یا تسلی

314

The woman caught under allegation of the of theft in Era of Prophet (PBUH) belongs to tribe of ________

Correct Answer: Banu Makhzm
Show explanation
Explanation

The woman caught under allegation of the of theft in Era of Prophet (PBUH) belongs to tribe of Banu Makhzm۔

The Prophet said, "The hand should be cut off for stealing something that is worth a quarter of a Dinar or more."

315

The first degree of faith is to stop evil by ______

Correct Answer: hand
Show explanation
Explanation

The first degree of faith is to stop evil by hand

ابو سعید خدری رضی اللہ عنہ سے روایت ہے، وہ کہتے ہیں کہ میں نے اللہ کے رسولﷺ کو فرماتے ہوئے سنا ہے 

’’تم میں سے جو شخص کوئی برائی دیکھے، وہ اسے اپنے ہاتھ سے بدل (روک) دے۔ اگر وہ اس کی طاقت نہیں رکھتا، تو پھر اپنی زبان سے بدل دے۔ اگر وہ اس کی بھی طاقت نہیں رکھتا، تو پھر اسے اپنے دل میں برا جانے اوریہ ایمان کا کم تر درجہ ہے۔‘‘ 

316

The Basic unit of society is _____________.

Correct Answer: Home
Show explanation
Explanation

The Basic unit of society is the home.

alose remember:

The family is the basic unit of society

verified from HEC answer key


317

The Stoning of the Devil ritual is performed at the Jamarat, which is located in _________ near Mecca.

Correct Answer: Mina
Show explanation
Explanation

The Stoning of the Devil ritual is performed at the Jamarat,

which is located in Mina, near Mecca.

It is performed on four consecutive days of the Hajj, the 10th, 11th, 12th, and 13th days of Dhul-Hijjah (the 12th Islamic Month).

On the first day, pilgrims cast seven stones at Jamarat al-Aqaba.

318

What is meant by Islam?

Correct Answer: see the answer
Show explanation
Explanation

The word “Islam” means “submission to the will of God.” 

The fundamental belief of Islam is “There is only one God, and Muhammad is his prophet.”

319

Which Angle Will Blow The Pig On The Day Of Judgment?

Correct Answer: Israfeel (A.S)
Show explanation
Explanation

 Hazrat Israfil A.S. is known as the angel who will blow the trumpet (sur) on the Day of Judgment (Yawm al-Qiyamah).

320

Who are Karaman Katebeen and Munkir Nakeer?

Correct Answer: see the answer in the detail
Show explanation
Explanation

Karaman & Katebeen , Munkir & Nakeer are angles

Karaman-Katebeen are two angels called Raqib and Atid

They have two angels, which sit on either shoulder to record a person's actions.

Munkar & Nakeer are two other angels

who are sent to the grave to test the faith of the dead persons.

321

Parts of wife from husband's inheritance?

Correct Answer: see the answer in the detail
Show explanation
Explanation

1/8th of men (if she has children)

1/4th of men  (if she doesn't have any children)

322

Who considered the Quran as the creature of Allah?

Correct Answer: Moatazila
Show explanation
Explanation

Moatazila معتزلہ

معتزلہ ایک عقلیت پسند فرقہ جس کا بانی ایک ایرانی نژاد واصل بن عطا الغزال شاگرد خواجہ حسن بصری تھا

اس کے نزدیک قرآن مخلوق ہے، توحید عقلاً معلوم ہو سکتی ہے، گناہ کبیرہ کا مرتکب کافر ہے وغیرہ وغیرہ،

مامون الرشید کے عہد میں یہ سرکاری مذہب بن گیا تھا۔


323

Israel was the son of which prophet?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Ishaq (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Yaqub was also known as Israel.
  • He is the son of Ishaq (father) and Rafqa (mother)‎
  • He was the grandson of Hazrat Ibrahim A.S.
  • Hazrat Ishaq was the younger brother of Hazrat Ismail A.S
  • He has 12 Children including Yusuf and Binyamin
  • His name mentioned 16 times in the Quran
  • He was born in Palestine,
  • He met his son Hazrat Yousaf (AS) after a period of 40 years
    • The Holy Qur'an has mentioned his story in a beautiful chapter entitled "Surah Yusuf".
324

Hazrat Sara (RA) was whose sister?

Correct Answer: Hazrat Loot (AS)
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Hazrat Saarah was the First wife of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S).
  • She was the mother of Hazrat Ishaq (A.S)
  •  Hazrat Saarah was  the sister of Prophet Loot
  •  The name of  Hazrat Saarah is mentioned in two suras of the Qur'an.
  • Hazrat Saarah is one of the Muhaddatha women (to whom the angels spoke).
  • Hazrat Saarah passed away long afterward at the age of 127 in Hebron (al-Khalil).
  • Sarah married her cousin, Ibrahim (a) when he was 37 years old.
325

Only _________Sahifay were revealed to Hazrat Ibraheem (AS).

Correct Answer: Three
Show explanation
Explanation
  • Only three Sahifay were revealed to Hazrat Ibraheem (AS)
  • Namrud ordered throwing Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) into the fire
  • Nimrod, the king of Babylon, commanded his men to throw Prophet Ibrahim (AS) into a huge pit of burning fire
  • Hazrat Ibrahim A.S remained on fire for 40 days
  • Khalil Ullah is the title of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S)
  • Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S) was born Iraq
  • Hazrat Ibrahim's (AH) name is mentioned in the Holy Quran 67 times.
    • Abraham had two children,
    • 1.      Ishaq 
    • 2.      Ismael
326

Where is Masjid-e-Nabvi located?

Correct Answer: Madina
Show explanation
Explanation
  • The Al-Masjid an-Nabawi is the second mosque built by the Islamic prophet Muhammad in Medina
  • مسجد نبوی is a mosque in the city of Madina in the Saudi region of the Hejaz
  • مسجد نبوی the second largest mosque and the holiest site in Islam
  • The construction of مسجد نبوی started in: 1 Hijir
  • مسجد نبوی was built by the Holy Prophet (PBUH) himself, next to the house in 622 AD (first Hijri).
  • On 29th Hijrah was مسجد نبوی expanded
  • Height: 104 m
  • Burials: Abu Bakr, Umar
  • Minaret(s): 10

327

Ghazwa Tabook was fought between _________?

Correct Answer: Romans and Muslim
Show explanation
Explanation

Ghazwa Tabook is fought between Romans and Muslim

This Battle is known as the last Ghazwa of the Holy Prophet

It was fought in the year: 9 Hijri

initiated by Muhammad in October 630 AD.

It took place During Rajab and Sha'ban in the region of Tabuk

20 days Hazrat Muhammad PBUH stayed during the Tabook Battle



328

How many sons of Hazrat Yaqoob A.S?

Correct Answer: 12
Show explanation
Explanation

Hazrat Yaqoob has 12 Children including Yusuf and Binyamin

Hazrat Yousaf (AS) and Hazrat Yaqoob (AS) met each other after a period of 40 years

*****

Yaqub was also known as Israel.

Ishaq (father) Rafqa (mother)‎

Resting place: Cave of the Patriarchs, Hebron


329

No war pact was concluded between Muslims and the Makkans at Hadaibiya for a period of ________ years?

Correct Answer: 10
Show explanation
Explanation

Sule e Huabaibiya Was Signed For 10 Years, In The Year 628/ 6H.

Hudabia is the name of the tree.

Sulah e Hudaibiya is called Fatah Al-Mubeen in Surah Al-Fatah


Additional Information

The Treaty of Hudaibiya took place between Muhammad and the Qurayshi tribe of Mecca 

Suhail bin Amar signed the Treaty on behalf of the Quraish Mecca.

Al-Hudaibiyah Treaty was scribed by Ali Al-Murtaza (R.A).

7 clauses did the treaty of Hudaybiyyah contain


ND-21-5-2023


330

Among the companions, ___________ knew Hebrew, Coptie and Persian Language.

Correct Answer: Zayd Bin Thabit
Show explanation
Explanation

Zayd had been commanded by Muhammad to learn Hebrew and Coptic

He was the personal scribe of the Islamic prophet Muhammad

He served as the chief recorder of the Quran text in one volume

Zayd was 11 years old when he asked permission to participate in the Battle of Badr

331

Within only ________ years of the Prophet's PBUH demise, the Islamic State has spread to three continents.

Correct Answer: 15
Show explanation
Explanation

Within only 15 years of the Prophet's PBUH demise the Islamic State

The Islamic conquests, which culminated in the Arab empire being established across three continents:

  • Asia
  • Africa
  • Europe

The spread of Islam spans about 1,400 years.



Additional Information:

محمد صلی اللہ علیہ وسلم کی وفات کے بعد مسلمانوں کی فتوحات خلافت کے قیام کا باعث بنیں۔

ایک وسیع جغرافیائی علاقے پر قبضہ؛ عرب مسلم افواج نے وسیع علاقوں کو فتح کرنے اور وقت کے ساتھ ساتھ سامراجی ڈھانچے کی تعمیر سے اسلام قبول کرنے کو فروغ دیا۔

***

ND-21-5-2023

332

Warqa bin Naufal was the follower of ________ at the time of the first revelation of the Prophet of Islam.

Correct Answer: Christianity
Show explanation
Explanation

Warqa bin Naufil was considered a Christian priest. 

Warqa bin Naufil also known as  ‘Abdul ‘Uzza,

He was the first man to verify Prophet's first time

He used to write the writing with Hebrew letters.

He was the paternal cousin of Khadijah bint Khuwaylid.

333

What is the Qur’anic argument on the Life Hereafter? What are its impacts on the individual and collective life of a human being?

Correct Answer: see the answer in detail
Show explanation
Explanation

What is the Qur’anic argument on the Life Hereafter? What are its impacts on the individual and collective life of a human being? (20 marks)

Qur'anic Argument on the Life Hereafter

  1. Purpose and Accountability: The Qur'an states that life is a test and that humans are created to worship and obey God. Accountability in the Hereafter ensures every action is rewarded or punished.

    • "Do you think that We created you in play (without any purpose)?" (Qur'an 23:115).

  2. Resurrection and Divine Justice: The Qur'an highlights resurrection as a demonstration of God's power, ensuring ultimate justice.

    • "And to Allah belongs the Hereafter and the former (life)" (Qur'an 53:25).
    • "The Day when everyone will be paid in full for what they have earned" (Qur'an 3:185).

  3. Natural Signs as Evidence: The Qur'an frequently points to signs in nature (e.g., plants revived from dead soil) as evidence of resurrection.

    • "Look at the earth, how We bring life to it after its death" (Qur'an 30:19).
Impacts on Individual Life
  1. Moral Accountability: Awareness of judgment day fosters honesty, integrity, and a strong moral compass.
  2. Encouragement of Good Deeds: Promotes acts of charity, kindness, and patience due to the promise of reward.
  3. Inner Peace: Belief in divine justice brings comfort in facing life's hardships and injustices.
Impacts on Collective Life
  1. Promotion of Justice and Social Welfare: Encourages fair dealings and the establishment of justice in society.
  2. Strengthening of Social Bonds: Compassion and charity are encouraged, creating trust and support within communities.
  3. Discouragement of Social Vices: Reduces crimes, corruption, and exploitation by instilling fear of accountability.

In essence, belief in the Hereafter shapes ethical, social, and spiritual behaviors, guiding both individuals and communities toward a just and compassionate society


334

Companions of the Holy Prophet at the Bait-e-Shajrah were _________?

Correct Answer: 1400
Show explanation
Explanation

Bait e Rizwan, also known as Bait e Sharjah

On this occasion, Muhammad gathered his nearly 1,400 Sahaba 

This bait sworn to the Prophet by his Sahaba prior to the Treaty of Hudaybiyyah

Bait Rizwan was the pledge taken to get revenge on Hazrat Usman (RA) 

This bait is made under the Keekar tree.




335

Self Respect to mankind is given by whom?

Correct Answer: Islam
Show explanation
Explanation

Self-respect for humankind is granted by Tauheed.

    The six Sunni articles of belief are:

    1. Belief in God and tawhid (monotheism)
    2. Belief in the angels
    3. Belief in the Islamic holy books
    4. Belief in the prophets and messengers
    5. Belief in the Last Judgment and Resurrection
    6. Belief in predestination

    The first five are based on several Quranic beliefs
    336

    What was the kuniyat of Hazrat Abu Bakr (RA)?

    Correct Answer: Abu Abdullah
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Abu Bakr's kunniyat was  Abu Abdullah 
    he was the father-in-law of the Islamic prophet Muhammad
    his daughter Aisha married with  prophet Muhammad
    His real name was Abdullah Ibn Abi Quhafa.
    First Caliph Hazrat Abu Bakr Siddiq (R.A)
    He belonged to the Tribe: Quraysh (Banu Taym)
    His father's name is Uthman Abu Quhafa.
    His Mother's name is Salma Ummul-Khair.
    Place of burial: Al Masjid an Nabawi, Al-Madinah al-Munawwarah, Saudi Arabia
    337

    Humama bin Kharsha was the disciple of _______

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Abu Bakar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Humama bin Kharsha was the disciple of Hazrat Abu Bakar

    338

    بنی اسرائیل جس بچھڑے کی پوجا کرتے تھے اس کی آواز کس سے ملتی تھی؟

    Correct Answer: بچھڑے کی آواز
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     سیّدنا موسیٰ علیہ السّلام کے جانے کے 30دن بعد سامِری نے بنی اسرائیل سے زیورات جمع کئے اور انہیں پگھلا کر ایک بچھڑا بنادیا۔

    اس کے پاس اُس جگہ کی مٹّی تھی جس جگہ حضرتِ سیّدنا جبریل علیہ السّلام کے گھوڑے نے قدم رکھا تھا۔

    اس نے جیسے ہی وہ مٹّی بے جان بچھڑے میں ڈالی تو وہ گائے کی طرح آواز نکالنے لگا۔

      بچھڑے کی پوجا اِس کے بعد سامِری نے لوگوں کو بہکانا شروع کردیا کہ یہ بچھڑا تمہارا اور موسیٰ علیہ السّلام کا خدا ہے

    339

    اللہ تعالی نے سب چیزوں کے نام کس کو سیکھاے؟

    Correct Answer: حضرت آدم
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    سورہ بقرہ آیت 31 میں حضرت آدم علیہ السلام کے حوالے سے یہ بیان ہوا ہے کہ انھیں اللہ تعالیٰ نے تمام اسماء سکھائے تھے

    340

    The book "Khilafat o Malukiyat" is authored by?

    Correct Answer: Abul Ala Maududi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    341

    Faran valley is situated in

    Correct Answer: Makkah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Faran valley is situated in Makkah.


    342

    Zakat collectors were directed by prophet (PBUH) to collect Zakat on animals by

    Correct Answer: Visiting the place where the cattle were kept by the owner
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Nisab when Zakat becomes applicable is forty animals that are more than twelve months old.

    There is no Zakat if the number is less than forty.

    How many camels render are to par Zakat? 5

    How many sheep render are to pay zakat? 40

    Zakat is payable on gold of_________7.5 tolas

    Zakat is payable on silver of_______52.5 tolas

    343

    Herd of Grazing goat up to which number was exempt from zakat ?

    Correct Answer: 39 goats
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Nisab when Zakat becomes applicable is forty animals that are more than twelve months old.

    There is no Zakat if the number is less than forty.

    How many camels render are to par Zakat? 5

    How many sheep render are to pay zakat? 40

    Zakat is payable on gold of_________7.5 tolas

    Zakat is payable on silver of_______52.5 tolas

    344

    Which forum can decide under Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 as amended, the question whether a declaration about exemption from zakat made under section 3 is valid according to the fiqah declarant profess to follow :

    Correct Answer: Federal Court
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Federal Shariat Court may, on the application of any person, decide the

    question whether a declaration such as is referred to in the first or second proviso to

    subsection (3) made by any person is valid according to the fiqh he professes to follow; and,

    if the Federal Shariat Court decides that the declaration is not valid, such person shall,

    without prejudice to any other action that may be, taken against him under any other law, the

    liable to pay Zakat or Ushr as the case may be, in the manner laid down in this Ordinance.

    345

    Usher shall be charged and collected on compulsory basis only from:

    Correct Answer: Land owner in cultivating possession
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ushr is collected on compulsory basis at a rate of 5 percent of the produce from every landowner.

    unless they fail to meet the definition of sahib-e-nisab,

    producing more than 849 kilograms of wheat, or its equivalent in value.


    346

    The landowner would be exempt from payment of Usher if the produce from his land is less than:

    Correct Answer: 948 kilograms of wheat or its equivalent
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     The produce from his land is less than five wasqs (=948 Kilograms) of wheat.

    Or its equivalent in value in the case of other crops liable to Ushr. 

    347

    What is the serial number of the kalimah Rad-e-Kufer among the 6 Kalimah?

    Correct Answer: sixth
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The serial number of the 6 Kalimah is sixth.

    The name of the sixth Kalimah is Kalimah Radd-i-Kufar

    six kalimbas are recited in Nikah

    List of six Kalimas

    1

    كَلِمَاتْ اَلطَّيِّبَة‎

    (Word of Purity)

    2

    كَلِمَاتْ اَلشَّهَادَة‎

    (Word of Testimony)

    3

    كَلِمَاتْ اَلتَّمْجِيدْ‎

    (Word of Majesty)

    4

    كَلِمَاتْ اَلتَّوْحِيدْ‎

    (Word of Oneness)

    5

    كَلِمَاتْ إِسْتِغْفَارْ‎

    (Word of Penitence)

    6

    كَلِمَاتْ رَدّْ اَلْكُفْرْ‎

    ("Word of Rejection of Disbelief")

    348

    Tayammum (Ablution with sand) order is in surah?

    Correct Answer: Maidah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Tayammum (Ablution with sand) order is in surah Maidah.

    Wazu (Ablution) was ordered on  5 A.H

    The acts which break the ablution are called Nawaqiz-e-Wazu

    There are 6 Sunnah acts of ablution

    There are 4 obligations for ablution.

    13 Sunnah are in four obligations of Ablution

    By Hazrat Jibrail (علیہ), Prophet ﷺ learnt Salat & ablution

    وضو کے چار فرائض ہیں۔

    نمبر 1: چہرہ دھونا

    نمبر 2: دونوں ہاتھوں کو کہینیوں تک دھونا

    نمبر 3: چوتھائی سر کا مسح کرنا

    نمبر 4:پاوں کا ٹخنیوں سمیت دھونا

    ****


    349

    The Naat "Balaghal Ula Bikamalehi" is written by?

    Correct Answer: Sheikh Saadi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    350

    Pharaoh and his troops were drowned in?

    Correct Answer: Red Sea
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Pharaoh changes his mind and gives chase until he corners the fugitives at the Red Sea, whereupon Moses, at God's command, causes the waters to part so that the Israelites can pass safely across

    351

    Al Abwa is situated betwen

    Correct Answer: Makkah & Madinah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Al-Abwā'  is a Hejazi village between Mecca and Medina belonging to the area of Rabigh, on the western coast of Saudi Arabia.

    The Islamic Prophet Muhammad entered it before the Battle of Badr, in 2 Safar A.H.

    352

    The Pilgrims are gathered in Arafat on:

    Correct Answer: 9th Zilhajj
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    9th of Dhu'l-Hijjah: This is the Day of Arafat.


    . After performing the dawn (Fajr) prayer at Mina, leave for Arafat on the morning of the 9th of Dhu'l-Hijjah.

    353

    Mubahila verse is in surah?

    Correct Answer: Al-Imran
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Al-Mubāhala verse is verse 61 of Chapter 3 of the Holy Qur'an (Sura Al 'Imran) which is about the Prophet's (s) encounter with Christians of Najran.

    354

    Who suggested to dig a ditch around city of Medina?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Salman Farsi
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    1. On the recommendation of Hazrat SALMAN FARSI, a trench was dug around the base of MEDINA to protect all the approaches.
    2. The pagans of MECCA had failed to annihilate the Muslim base of MEDINA in the battle of UHUD, SHAW AL 3 HURA.
    355

    Order of Fasting in Ramadhan was revealed in?

    Correct Answer: 2nd Hijri
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    356

    Muqawqis was the king of?

    Correct Answer: Egypt
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Al-Muqawqis is mentioned in Islamic history as a ruler of Egypt

    He is widely identified as the last prefect of Egypt.

    357

    The literal meaning of 'Qiyas' is

    Correct Answer: To assess
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    358

    The Prophet Muhammad advised the name of Hazrat Abu Bakr after embracing Islam as

    Correct Answer: Abdullah
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    359

    In 14 Hijri 635 A.D. _________ laid the foundation of Basrah.

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar (R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Second caliph of Islam Hazrat Umar (RA) laid the foundation of Basra in 635 AD (14th Hijir) 

    Basra is an Iraqi city located on the Shatt al-Arab.

    360

    The king of Ajam was send which one to the Hazrat Muhammad?

    Correct Answer: Kabeeb
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    361

    What was the kunniyat of Hazrat Ayesha (R.A.)?

    Correct Answer: Umme Abdullah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Aisha RA was given the Kunya – Umme Abdullah on her insistence to the Prophet ﷺ and she was nicknamed by him as Humaira' or the 'Little Red One' which was basically to emphasize her beauty as is also mentioned in numerous Hadith

    362

    Name the Sahabi who served meal to the guests at the time of iftar and he itself break his fast from water?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Ali
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    363

    Who Sahabi devoted the water well which bought from Jews for the Muslims?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Usman Ghani
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    364

    'Ibadat' is derived from which Arabic word?

    Correct Answer: Ibad
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    365

    At the time of conquer of Makkah, 1st man who embraced Islam?

    Correct Answer: Abu Sufyan ibn Haris
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Abu Sufyan ibn Harb converts to IslamUpon their arrival, the Muslims were furious at the sight of Abu Sufyan and sought to kill him.

    366

    What is meant by Taqrir in the field of Hadith?

    Correct Answer: speech
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hadith also means the reported speech or taqreer of the successors to the companions of the Messenger of Allah.

    1. ISNAD (Sequence of Reporters) – This is the chain of narrators through which the Hadith has spread.
    2. TARAF (Introductory Text) – This is the beginning of the text which refers to the actions or characteristics of the Holy Prophet ﷺ.
    3. MATN (Content) – This is the main text of the Hadith, or the actual speech of the Prophet ﷺ.


    367

    Masallamah al Kazaab was?

    Correct Answer: False Prophet
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Musaylimah bin Habīb later known as Musaylimah Al-Kazzab (The liar)

    Musayliama the Liar  d.632, from the Banu Hanifa tribe

    Masallamah al Kazaab claimed to be a prophet during the lifetime of Muhammad (PBUH).

    Masallamah al Kazaab was a leader during the Ridda wars.

    ND15-10-2023



    368

    The traditionalists apply ________ methods for the authenticity of Hadis

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are three primary ways to determine the authenticity (sight) of a hadith:

    1. by attempting to determine whether there are "other identical reports from other transmitters";
    2. determining the reliability of the transmitters of the report;
    3. and "the continuity of the chain of transmission" of the hadith.
    369

    When did Zakat, Ushar, and Hudood ordinances enforced?

    Correct Answer: 1979-80
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The zakah and user system in Pakistan was introduced through the Zakat and Ushr (Organisation) Ordinance, 1979, issued on June 24, 1979, for the establishment of zakah organizations at:

    national,

    provincial,

    District,

    subdistrict, and local (village/ward) levels.

    Subsequently, the Zakat and Ushr Ordinance, 1980 was promulgated on June 20, 1980. 

    **

    In Pakistan, the system of compulsory collection and distribution of Zakat and Ushr began in 1980 with an ordinance decreed by General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq 

    Zakat and Usher Ordinance Question:

    Zakat and Usher Ordinance can be challenged in the civil court

    The prime objective of Zakat and Usher is To assist the needy

    All members of Zakat and Usher Councils & Committees are Muslim

    District Zakat and Usher committee was constituted under section 14

    According to section 16(7) of Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980, the Chairman and members of the District Zakat and Usher committee shall hold office for a term of 3 years

    Sheep grazing fed free in pastures are exempted from Zakat according to the Zakat and Usher Ordinance 

    370

    When first Namaz of Eid ul Fitr was read?

    Correct Answer: 1st Shawal, 2 Hijri
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    first Namaz of Eid ul Fitr was read on 1st Shawal, 2 Hijri

    first Eid Prayer offered by the Holy prophet in 2nd A.H

    Eid literally means a “festival” or “feast” in Arabic. 

    Doing ghusl on Eid is mustahabb. 

    It was narrated that the Prophet ﷺ did ghusl on the day of Eid. 

    Eid Prayer is Wajib

    Eid Salat consists of two 'Rakats' and six 'Takbirs'.



    371

    Qalala means?

    Correct Answer: One Having Neither Parents nor Childern
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Qalala means One Having Neither Parents nor Children

    verse 4:176 is a special case of kalala where there is no spouse (because a kalala can have a spouse).


    372

    In which Surah of the Quran are the details of heritage?

    Correct Answer: Surah E Nisa
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The detailed explanations about heirs and their shares in the Quran take place in a chapter of Nisa/4 verses no. 11-12 and 176.

    These verses set up the basic foundations of the inheritance law of Islam.

    373

    Which lady name came in Quran?

    Correct Answer: Maryam A.S
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Maryam (Mary) is the only woman named in the Quran.

    She is mentioned by name 31 times

    Her story is told in three Meccan surahs (19, 21, 23) and four Medinan surahs (3, 4, 5, 66). 

    She is the mother of Jesus and the sister of Aaron. 

    She is described as one of the greatest women of all time in the Quran. 

    The nineteenth Surah, Maryam, is named after her. 

    374

    Write down the Names of 6 books of hadiths.

    Correct Answer: see the answer in the detail
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Q: Write down the Names of 6 books of hadiths.

    the Six Canonical Books of Hadith commonly known as 'Sehay-e-Sittah'

    1. Sahih al-Bukhari
    2. Sahih Muslim
    3. Sunan Abu Dawood
    4. Sunan al-Tirmidhi
    5. Sunan al-Nasa'i
    6. Sunan ibn Majah
    375

    Write down the fundamental principles of Islam.

    Correct Answer: see the answer in the detail
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    These five fundamental principles of an Islamic system are of Islam:

    1) Shahadatain (Touheed) (Kalmah)

    2) Salat

    3) Zakat

    4) Soam

    5) Hajj 

    376

    Lambardar is :

    Correct Answer: A non salaried public servant appointed by collector
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Lambardar is a non salaried public servant appointed by collector 

    377

    Under Zakat and Ushr Ordinance 1980, Istehqaq for treatement through public hospital is determined by :

    Correct Answer: District Zakat and Ushr committee
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Under Zakat and Ushr Ordinance 1980, Istehqaq for treatement through public hospital is determined by District Zakat and Ushr committee 

    378

    The minimum age limit for female members of different Zakat committees is

    Correct Answer: 45 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The minimum age limit for female members of different Zakat committees is 45 years 

    379

    Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 vest the power to make rules in :

    Correct Answer: Federal government
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 vest the power to make rules in Federal government 

    380

    The chief executive in respect of matters relating to Zakat and Usher is

    Correct Answer: Chairman of central Zakat council
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The chief executive in respect of matters relating to Zakat and Usher is Chairman of central Zakat council

    381

    The proceeds of Ushr are to be deposited in

    Correct Answer: P.L.A of district Zakat fund
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     proceeds of Ushr to be deposited  in the PLA of District Zakat Fund;. (iv) transfers, if any, from the Local Zakat Funds

    Introduction Zakat: One of the five pillars of Islam.

    Only imposed on Muslims, it is generally described as a 2.5% tax on savings to be donated to the Muslim poor and needy.

    Ushr: A 5% tax on the harvests of irrigated land and 10% tax on harvest from rain-watered land

    382

    Abdullah yousaf is famous for being

    Correct Answer: English trnslator and commentator of Holy Quran
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    One of the translators of the Qur'an as well as the Indian Mufasir Abdullah Yusuf Ali with his expertise seen in translating and interpreting the Qur'an.

    His work The Holy Qur'an Text Translation and Commentary .

    his most famous work has made his name known in the Muslim world.


    383

    which of following terms has been used in a particular quranic verse to denote the funds meant assist eight categories of Zakat beneficiaries

    Correct Answer: Sadaqat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Arabic term sadaqa (plural: sadaqaat) is used in the Quran to denote the funds meant to assist the poor and the contributions collected by the Islamic Authority to be spent for the purposes defined in the Quran.

    384

    Payment of Zakat for Shahb e Nisab Muslim is

    Correct Answer: Binding religious duty
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Zakat is not permissible to be spent on the construction of a mosque.
    • This is because it does not fit under any of the eight categories
    • List of Head of Zakat

    1. Al-Fuqara (The poor)

    2. Al-Masakin (The needy)

    3. Zakat collectors

    4. Who converted to Islam

    5. Freeing captives

    6. Debtors

    7. In the path of Allah

    8. Wayfarer (Traveler)

    Nisab of zakat for silver is 52 1/2 tola

    Zakat literally means Purification.

    When Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H (properly implemented in 8 Hijri.

    The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.

    Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat.

    Without which act the prayer is useless? Zakat

    One who disbelievers in Zakat is a Kafir


    “Zakat” is the treasure of Islam, who said this? Holy Prophet (PBUH)

    385

    Central Zakat Council shall consist of a chairman and four persons to be nominated by the president, of whom there shall be Ulema nominated in consultation with the

    Correct Answer: Council of Islamic ideology
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Central Zakat Council shall consist of a chairman and four persons to be nominated by the president, of whom there shall be Ulema nominated in consultation with the Council of Islamic ideology  

    386

    In section 2 of Zakt and Ushr Ordinance 1980, "Nisab" means :

    Correct Answer: 612.32 grams of silver
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Nisab of zakat for silver is 52 1/2 tola

    Zakat literally means Purification.

    When Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H (properly implemented in 8 Hijri.

    The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.

    Hazrat Abu Baker did Jihad on the issue of Zakat.

    Without which act the prayer is useless? Zakat

    One who disbelievers in Zakat is a Kafir


    “Zakat” is the treasure of Islam, who said this? Holy Prophet (PBUH)

    387

    If Ushr is not paid within time the local committee may adopt the first measure for recovery of Ushr demand viz

    Correct Answer: personal contact with the defaulter
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    If Ushr is not paid within time the local committee

    may adopt the first measure for recovery of Ushr demand personal contact with the defaulter 

    388

    Administrator General of Usher and Zakat at Federal level is appointed by

    Correct Answer: Ministry of religious affairs
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Administrator General of Usher and Zakat at Federal level is appointed by Ministry of religious affairs

    389

    According to Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980, Chief administrator in a province shall be appointed by the Governor in consultation with the

    Correct Answer: Federal Government
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to  Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980,

    Chief administrator in a province shall be appointed by the Governor in consultation with the Federal Government 

    390

    The Chairman and members of a District and Zakat committee shall cease to hold office , if it is proved in an inquiry held by provincial council that the district committee

    Correct Answer: Has a majority of members who are not pious Muslims or who are engaged in political activity
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Chairman and members of a District and Zakat committee shall cease to hold office , if it is proved in an inquiry held by provincial council that the district committee  has a majority of members who are not pious Muslims or who are engaged in political activity  

    391

    An individual land owner shall be exempt from the compulsory levy of Usher according to section 5 of the Zakat and Ushr ordinance 1980 if :

    Correct Answer: The production from his land is less than 948 kilograms of wheat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    An individual land owner shall be exempt from the compulsory levy of Usher according to section 5 of the Zakat and Ushr ordinance 1980

    if the production from his land is less than 948 kilograms of wheat 

    392

    The Arabic word Zakat has been derived from Zaka which means :

    Correct Answer: Purify
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The word zakah also holds the senses of “justness, integrity, and honesty,” along with “justification and vindication,”

    while zaka means to “sweeten” something.

    The use of the word zakat 

    which comes from the three Arabic root letters za • ka • wa and also za • ka • ya


    393

    Arrears of Usher shall be recovered by :

    Correct Answer: Tehsildar of revenue department
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Arrears of Usher shall be recovered by Tehsildar of revenue department 

    394

    A landowner got the produce of 1800 kilograms, of millet from his land. The notified value of millet was half that of the wheat. Under section 5 of the Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 he would be liable to pay Usher?

    Correct Answer: Ninety Kg of Millet
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    A landowner got the produce of 1800 kilograms, of millet from his land.

    The notified value of millet was half that of the wheat.

    Under section 5 of the Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 he would be liable to pay Usher Ninety Kg of Millet 

    395

    The goods meant for trading were :

    Correct Answer: Liable to pay Zakat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The goods meant for trading were liable to pay Zakat 

    396

    Zakat prescribed in respect of a buried treasure discovered from an uninhabited place during early Muslim period was

    Correct Answer: one fifth of the total proceeds
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat prescribed in respect of a buried treasure discovered from an uninhabited place during early Muslim period was one fifth  of the total proceeds 

    397

    The number of Ruku in the last chapter of Quran are

    Correct Answer: 39
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    There are 558 Rukus as a whole in the Holy Quran.

    Ruku comes from an Arabic word رُكوع.

    It is some part or paragraph in the Quran.

    During the revelation of the Holy Quran, it was not sub-divided by Rukus.

     Rukuas were not there in the reign of Prophet Muhammad (SAW) and Sahaaba (R.A). 

    These subdivisions were created during the reign of Hajaaj bin Yusuf.

    The number of Ruku in the last chapter of Quran are thirty nine 

    398

    The land owner would be exempt from payment of usher if the produce from his land is less than

    Correct Answer: 948 kilogram of wheat or its equivalent
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The land owner would be exempt from payment of usher if the produce from his land is less than 948 kilogram of wheat or its equivalent 

    the produce from his land is less than five wasqs (=948 Kilograms) of wheat, or its equivalent in value in the case of other crops liable to Ushr.

    399

    Usher shall be charged and collected on compulsory basis shall be from

    Correct Answer: Every land owner, grantee,allottee, lessee,leaseholder or landholder
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Usher shall be charged and collected on compulsory basis shall be from every land owner, grantee,allottee, lessee,leaseholder or landholder  

    400

    Which forum can decide under Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 as amended, the question whether a declaration about exemption from zakat made under section 3 is valid according to the fiqh declarant profess to follow

    Correct Answer: Federal Shariat Court
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Federal Shariat Court can decide under Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 as amended,

    the question whether a declaration about exemption from zakat made under section 3 is valid according to the fiqh declarant  profess to follow 

    401

    Who had been appointing as a governor of Yaman with the responsibility of collecting Zakat by the Prophet Muhammad.

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Maaz bin Jabal
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muʿādh ibn Jabal was a sahabi of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

    He was called by Muhammad "the one who will lead the scholars into Paradise." 

    Born: 605 AD, Al-Madinah al-Munawwarah, Saudi Arabia

    Died: 639 AD, North Shuna, Jordan

    402

    A government owned corporation earns a net profit of rupees one billion in a particular year. It will be liable to pay Zakat amounting to :

    Correct Answer: Zero
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat is payable at 2.5% of the wealth one possesses above the nisab.

    Nisab, which is equal to 85 grams of 24k gold.

    The minimum amount of wealth one must have before they are liable to pay zakat.

    403

    Who has authority to exempt any person from zakat in an Islamic state

    Correct Answer: None of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    urah Tooba describes 8 heads of Zakat.

    List of Head of Zakat
     1. Al-Fuqara (The poor)
    2. Al-Masakin (The needy)
    3. Zakat collectors
    4. Who converted to Islam
    5. Freeing captives
    6. Debtors
    7. In the path of Allah
    8. Wayfarers (Travelers)


    32 times the word Zakat occurs in the Holy Quran



    22 times word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs

    Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H.


    but its complete system was imposed in the year of 8th hijrah.


    404

    Land watered by buckets, during the period of Holy Prophet were taxed ___ .

    Correct Answer: 5 %
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    10% rain water irrigated land
     Arid 20% if land is irrigated by canal water.
    405

    Honey if an article of trade, is taxable of ___ % zakat.

    Correct Answer: 10
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Honey if an article of trade, is taxable of 10 % zakat.

    406

    Pasturing Oxen, less than __ are Zakat free .

    Correct Answer: 30
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    When the number of cows is greater,

    the zakat due is calculated by multiples of these figures:

    a one-year-old cow for every 30, and a two-year-old cow for every 40.

    For sheep: For 40-120 sheep, one sheep is due in zakat for 121-200, two sheep become due.

    For 201-300, three sheep are due.


    407

    Which category of the following can be given Zakat ?

    Correct Answer: All of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr

    , which, in technical usage, refers to Zakat al-Fitr.

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr, which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    408

    For the orphaned child, without wealth responsibility of payment of Fatrana lies on ___

    Correct Answer: man or woman on whom he depends
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr,

    Also known as Zakat al-Fitr.

    or the special Zakat,

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr,

    which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    409

    Zakat of Eid ul Fitr of insane person is

    Correct Answer: Not compulsory
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr, which, in technical usage, refers to Zakat al-Fitr.

    or the special Zakat,

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr, which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    410

    Which kind of food is acceptable for payment of Fatrana ?

    Correct Answer: All of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr, which, in technical usage, refers to Zakat al-Fitr.

    or the special Zakat,

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr, which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    411

    Whether food stuffs of bad quality can be ordered as Fatrana ?

    Correct Answer: Certainly not
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr,

    which, in technical usage, refers to Zakat al-Fitr.

    or the special Zakat,

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr, which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    412

    When Zakat of Eid ul Fitr must be satisfied at the latest

    Correct Answer: During the Eid prayer
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr, which, in technical usage, refers to Zakat al-Fitr.

    or the special Zakat,

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr, which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    413

    Zakat of Eid ul Fitr is applicable on new born infants ____.

    Correct Answer: Born on Chand rat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr, which, in technical usage, refers to Zakat al-Fitr.

    or the special Zakat,

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr, which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    414

    Fatrana on Muslims newly converted to the faith is ___

    Correct Answer: Compulsory
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr,

    Also called Zakat al-Fitr.

    or the special Zakat,

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr, which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    415

    Who is supposed to pay Fatrana of minor children ?

    Correct Answer: Father
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fitrana is the Urdu word for the Arabic al-fitr, which, in technical usage, refers to Zakat al-Fitr.

    or the special Zakat,

    or obligatory alms, of “Breaking the Fast” at the end of the month of Ramadan.

    “Fitrana” refers specifically to the name of the obligatory payment, called fitrah in Arabic.

    It derives from the Arabic word fitr, which literally means “the ‘nature’ upon which God created the human being.”




    416

    Camels less than ___ are zakat free as established by the Holy Prophet .

    Correct Answer: 5
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Camels less than 5 are zakat free as established by the Holy Prophet .

    417

    Which animal of following is not taxable for Zakat ?

    Correct Answer: Elephant
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Elephant is not taxable for Zakat

    418

    How much usher is paid when crop is destroyed in disaster ?

    Correct Answer: No ushr is paid
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    No ushr is paid  is paid when crop is destroyed in disaster 

    Crop destruction is the deliberate destruction of crops or agricultural products to render it useless for consumption or processing.

    It can be made by burning, grinding, dumping into water, or application of chemicals.


    419

    Name of zakat most of time is repeated with

    Correct Answer: Salat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Of the 30 places the word Zakat occurs in the Quran

    In 27 of them it is closely paired with its “sister Pillar” of Islam, the Ṣalât-Prayer .


    420

    Zakat funds its emphasis both from ___

    Correct Answer: Quran and Sunnah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat funds its emphasis both from Quran and Sunnah 

    421

    Representative of woman in Local Committee is ____ .

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    According to the Punjab Zakat and Ushr Act 2018

    The Local Zakat and Ushr Committee shall consist of:

    1. Chairman
    2. nine members, including at least three Muslim women
    422

    All members of Zakat and Usher Councils & Committees are ___

    Correct Answer: Muslims
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    All members of Zakat and Usher Councils & Committees are Muslims 

    423

    Local Zakat and Ushr Committee consist of __ members .

    Correct Answer: 9
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Local Zakat and Ushr Committee consist of 9 members. 

    424

    Usher is collected in ____

    Correct Answer: cash
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ushr shall be collected in cash, except in case of wheat or paddy, for which, Ushr, at the option of the Council, may be collected in kind.

    425

    Central Zakat Council consists of ____ Ulema .

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat Councils are responsible for collecting and distributing the Islamic taxes known as Zakat and Ushr in Pakistan.

    426

    Blair House is an official guest house for foreign dignitaries. In which country it is situated ?

    Correct Answer: USA
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Blair House, also known as The President's Guest House, is an official residence in Washington, D.C.

     The President's Guest House has been called "the world's most exclusive hotel" .

    Parts of the historic complex have been used for an official residence since the 1940s.

    427

    In administration under the Holy prophet Zakat was levied on :

    Correct Answer: None of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The zakat is levied on five categories of property.

    Food grains

    fruit

    camels

    cattle

    sheep

     goats; gold and silver; and movable goods—and is payable each year after one year's possession.

    428

    O prophet receive Zakat from their properties to that could make them neat and clean. In which surah of Quran this Ayat was revealed ?

    Correct Answer: surah Muhammad
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muhammad is the 47th chapter.

     The title is derived from the direct mentioning of the Islamic prophet Muhammad in 47:2.

    It also has the name of Al-Qitāl,

    which translates to fighting due to the context of the sura. 

    No. of Rukus: 4

    No. of verses: 38

    429

    Voluntary donations are called

    Correct Answer: Atiyyas
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    words related to donation, such as:

    aid,

    allowance,

    appropriation,

    assistance,

    bequest and charity.

    430

    Who are exempted from paying Zakat ?

    Correct Answer: All of above
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The poor, the indigent, the debt-ridden and the unfree.

    While gold and silver are Zakat eligible, there is no Zakat due on your personal belongings that you consider necessary.

    431

    The Chairman and its members are elected for the period of :

    Correct Answer: 3 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Term of office of the Chairman and the Deputy Chairman is three years.


    432

    The Chairman and its members are elected for the period of

    Correct Answer: 3 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Term of office of the Chairman and the Deputy Chairman is three years.


    433

    Zakat can be collected through

    Correct Answer: Banks branches
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat can be collected through Banks branches 

    434

    Who was the first levy Usher on land

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Umar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The term has also been used for a 10% tax on merchandise imported from states that taxed the Muslims on their products.

    Caliph `Umar ibn Al-Khattāb was the first Muslim ruler to levy ushr.


    435

    Who started compulsory collection of Zakat

    Correct Answer: Zia ul Haq
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Pakistan, the system of compulsory collection and distribution of Zakat and Ushr began in 1980.

    An ordinance decreed by General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq calling for a 2.5% annual deduction from personal bank accounts

    on the first day of Ramadan,

    with the revenue to be used for poverty relief.


    436

    Fi sabeelillah Means

    Correct Answer: For Allah only
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The phrase fi sabilillah is an Arabic expression meaning "in the cause of Allah", or more befittingly, "for the sake of Allah".

    437

    Ghermeen means

    Correct Answer: Debit ridden
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The word “ghareem” comes from the word “ghurm”, which means debt.

    The gharem is a person in debt, which is clearly stated in the Quran as an eligible category to receive Zakat.

    Therefore if a person has a debt, and cannot pay it back, he/ she can receive the amount needed to pay back the debt.


    438

    In a republic head of state is known as

    Correct Answer: President
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The head of a sovereign,

    independent state is usually identified as the person who,

    according to that state's constitution,

    is the reigning monarch, in the case of a monarchy;

    or the president, in the case of a republic.


    439

    The actual disbursement of Zakat is made through ___ .

    Correct Answer: Local Zakat Committee
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    This account will be credited with the amounts received as financial grants from government

    or from a semi-government or non-government.

    440

    The ministry of religious affairs, Zakat and Usher allocates funds to the provinces on the basis of :

    Correct Answer: Percentage of population
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Disbursement of Zakat takes place by transfer of funds from Central Zakat Fund to the Provincial Zakat Fund

    which in turn transfers it to District Zakat Committees of all the districts on population basis.

    Zakat fund is meant for the poor and deserving Muslims living below poverty line.

    441

    The ministry of religious affairs, Zakat and Usher allocates funds to the provinces on the basis of ________

    Correct Answer: Percentage of population
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Disbursement of Zakat takes place by transfer of funds from Central Zakat Fund to the Provincial Zakat Fund

    which in turn transfers it to District Zakat Committees of all the districts on population basis.

    Zakat fund is meant for the poor and deserving Muslims living below poverty line.

    442

    In Punjab there are __ district zakat committees .

    Correct Answer: 35
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    District Zakat Committees.

    Each of the district has one Zakat Committee which is notified by the Provincial Zakat Council

    443

    The procedure relating to recovery of Usher in case of Non payment under section 17 has been laid down in :

    Correct Answer: Zakat and Usher rules 1980
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The procedure relating to recovery of Usher in case of Non payment under section 17 has been laid down in Zakat and Usher rules 1980 

    444

    The Chairman of district zakat and ushr shall be

    Correct Answer: elected through secrete ballot by chairman of local committee
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Chairman and member of districts and zakat committee shall cease to hold office if it is proved in an inquiry held by provincial council that the district committee Has a majority of members who are not pious Muslims or who are engaged in political activity  

    445

    An individual land owner shall be exempt from the compulsory levey of Usher according to Section 5 of the Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 if

    Correct Answer: The production from his land is less than 948 kilo grams of wheat
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    An individual land owner shall be exempt from the compulsory levey of Usher

    according to Section 5 of the Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980 if the production from his land is less than 948 kilo grams of wheat 

    446

    Zakat year ends with the last day of ___

    Correct Answer: Shaban
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The zakat year begins on the date on which you were first in possession of wealth above the nisab

    447

    In clause of section 2 of Zakat and Usher Ordinance, 1980 Taluqa committee means :

    Correct Answer: A committee at Thana level constituted under section 17
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     In clause of section 2 of Zakat and Usher Ordinance, 1980 Taluqa committee

    means A committee at Thana level constituted under section 17

    448

    Zakat and Usher Ordinance ____

    Correct Answer: can be challenged in the civil court
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat and Usher Ordinance can be challenged in the civil court

    Zakat and Usher Ordinance Question:

    Zakat, Ushar, and Hudood ordinances enforced 1979-80

    In Pakistan, the system of compulsory collection and distribution of Zakat and Ushr began in 1980 with an ordinance decreed by General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq 

    The prime objective of Zakat and Usher is To assist the needy

    All members of Zakat and Usher Councils & Committees are Muslim

    District Zakat and Usher committee was constituted under section 14

    According to section 16(7) of Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980, the Chairman and members of the District Zakat and Usher committee shall hold office for a term of 3 years

    Sheep grazing fed free in pastures are exempted from Zakat according to the Zakat and Usher Ordinance 


    449

    What assets are exempted from Zakat according to Zakat and Usher Ordinance .

    Correct Answer: Sheep grazing fed free in pastures
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Sheep grazing fed free in pastures are exempted from Zakat according to the Zakat and Usher Ordinance 

    'assets' , means assets liable to Zakat as provided in this Ordinance

    There are eleven types of assets liable to Zakat "detailed in 1st schedule of Zakat & Ushr Ordinance 1980"

    There are four categories of people that do not pay the Zakat.

    These are the poor, the indigent, the debt-ridden, and the unfree.


    450

    The central Zakat council shall consist of Chairman and four persons to be nominated by the present, of whom there shall be Ulema nominated in consultation with the ____

    Correct Answer: Ministry of religious affairs
    Show explanation
    Explanation


    The zakah is levied on movable property if it remains in one's possession for one year, but the 'ushr is payable on each crop.

    The 'ushr is payable on the produce of the land even if the owner is a minor or a lunatic.

    451

    According to section 16(7) of Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980,the Chairman anf members of District Zakat and Usher committee shall hold office for a term of

    Correct Answer: 3 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Chairman and members of the Local Zakat and Ushr Committee shall hold office for a term of three years

    Zakat and Usher Ordinance Question:

    Zakat, Ushar, and Hudood ordinances enforced 1979-80

    In Pakistan, the system of compulsory collection and distribution of Zakat and Ushr began in 1980 with an ordinance decreed by General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq 

    The prime objective of Zakat and Usher is To assist the needy

    All members of Zakat and Usher Councils & Committees are Muslim

    District Zakat and Usher committee was constituted under section 14

    According to section 16(7) of Zakat and Usher Ordinance 1980, the Chairman and members of the District Zakat and Usher committee shall hold office for a term of 3 years

    Sheep grazing fed free in pastures are exempted from Zakat according to the Zakat and Usher Ordinance 




    452

    On cash gold and silver Zakat is paid at a rate of

    Correct Answer: 2 1/2 %
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    A related question about Zakat

    Zakat is the 3rd fundamental pillar of Islam.

    Zakat was made compulsory in 2 A.H but its complete system was imposed in the year of 8th hijrah.

    The word Zakat occurs 32 times in the Holy Quran.


    **

    How many camels render are to par Zakat? 5

    How many goats render are to pay zakat? 40

    How many cows render are to pay Zakat? 30-40

    How many bulls render to pay Zakat? 30-40

    How many buffaloes render to pay zakat? 30-40

    **

    Zakat is payable on gold of_________7.5 tolas

    Zakat is payable on silver of_______52.5 tolas


    How many times word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs? 22 times

    Who is not entitled to get Zakat? Parents, Husband, wife, and children

    453

    What was the age of Prophet Muhammad SAW when he married Hazrat Ayesha?

    Correct Answer: 53 years
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muhammad married Hazrat Ayesha when he was 53 years old and she was only six years old.

    He had intercourse with Hazrat Ayesha when she was nine years old.

    She was the 3rd wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

    She was the daughter of Hazrat Abu Baker 

    She was married to Muhammad after the Battle of Badr.

    She remained alive 46 years after the demise of Muhammad (PBUH).

    She gives the title of Siddiqa, meaning 'the truthful woman, daughter of the truthful man

    Hazrat Ayesha is also known for narrating 2,210 hadiths



    ND29-5-2023


    454

    Bab As Silsila is one of the gate of which mosque?

    Correct Answer: Al Aqsa Mosque
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Chain Gate (Bāb as-Silsila) is one of the gates to the Al-Aqsa Mosque compound on the Temple Mount in the Old City of Jerusalem. It was previously known as David's Gate. As it was named in the early Islamic period Bāb Daud which means David's Gate.

    455

    How many children Hazrat Yaqoob A.S had?

    Correct Answer: 12
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Yaqoob has 12 Children including Yusuf and Binyamin

    Hazrat Yousaf (AS) and Hazrat Yaqoob (AS) met each other after a period of 40 years


    Yaqub was also known as Israel.

    Ishaq (father) Rafqa (mother)‎

    Resting place: Cave of the Patriarchs, Hebron


    456

    In which battle 3 caliphs martyred due to the lake of water?

    Correct Answer: Khyber
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Ghazwa e khyber 3 caliphs were martyred due to the lake of water

    The Battle of Khaybar was fought in the year 628 between Muslims and the Jews living in the oasis of Khaybar,

    The Battle of Khaybar Start date: 629 AD
    The Battle of Khaybar Location:     Khaybar, Saudi Arabia
    The Battle of Khaybar End date:     629 AD
    The Battle of Khaybar Result:     Muslim victory

    How many Jews were killed in the Battle of Khyber? 93

    How many Muslims got martyrdom in the battle of Khyber? 15

    ND-5-09--023

    457

    How many verses in suah kausar?

    Correct Answer: 3
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    It is the 108th surah of the Quran and is one of the shortest surahs, with only three verses.

    The surah was revealed to Prophet Muhammad (peace be upon him) during the early days of his prophethood

    The meaning of Surah Al Kausar is Abundance.


    458

    What is the name of the mother of Hazrat Ismail (RA)?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Hajra
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Hazrat Hajirah was the second wife of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S).

    She was the mother of Hazrat Ismail (A.S)

    ****

    Hazrat Saarah was the First wife of Hazrat Ibrahim (A.S).

    She was the mother of Hazrat Ishaq (A.S)

    ND-5-09--023

    459

    Islam mean?

    Correct Answer: The religion of peace
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Islam is a religion of peace and mercy.

    It is a religion given and completed by Almighty Allah to Prophet Mohammed(P.b.u.h). 

    Islam means submission, surrender, and obedience.

    It believes in complete submission and obedience to Allah (The God).

    while

    There are five pillars of Islam:

    The first pillar of Islam is ____?

      Shahadatain 

    The second pillar of Islam is------?

      Namaz

    the third fundamental pillar of Islam?

      Zakat

    The fourth pillar of Islam is--------?

     Fasting (Soam)

    **

    5th pillar of Islam is?

      Hajj

    ****

    Which pillar of Islam is declared as armor?

     Fasting (Soam)

    Which pillar of Islam washes sin like water?

      Hajj

    ND-6-09-2023


    460

    The source of knowledge for Islamic education foundation based on

    Correct Answer: All of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    461

    Riyaz ul jannah is part of majid e ________ ?

    Correct Answer: Masjid Nabvi
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Rawdah Riyad ul-Jannah, inside the oldest part of Masjid an-Nabawi in Madina

    Riaz ul Jannah, also known as the “Garden of Paradise,”

    The Rawdah Riyad ul-Jannah is a small section between the pulpit (minbar) and the sacred chamber of Prophet Muhammad (SAW) called Riyazul Jannah

    The original size of Riyazul Jannah is approximately 22 meters in length


    NA21-8-2023

    462

    Founder of Bait ul muqadas?

    Correct Answer: Sulaymaan (A S)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Founder of Bait ul muqadas Sulaymaan (A S)

    Bait al-Maqdis, Bayt al-Maqdis, or Bayt al-Muqaddas  is an Arabic name and a common designation for Jerusalem in the Arabic language

    In 637 or 638, Caliph Umar traveled to Jerusalem in person to receive the submission of the city. The Patriarch thus surrendered to him.

    The Muslim conquest of the city solidified Arab control over Palestine, which would not again be threatened until the First Crusade in 1099.

    463

    House of_____ was given status of Dar ul Amaan during the time of the conquest of Makkah

    Correct Answer: Abu Sufyan
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    On the occasion of Fateh Makkah Abu Sufyan's house was described as Dar- ul-Aman by the Prophet (PBUH)

    The conquest of Makka in December 629 or January 630

    Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) "He who takes refuge in Abu Sufyan’s house is safe; whosoever confines himself to his house, the inmates thereof shall be in safety, and he who enters the Sacred Mosque is safe

    464

    When Zakat system was introduced in Pakistan_____?

    Correct Answer: 1980
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Zakat and Ushr began in 1980 with an ordinance decreed by General Muhammad Zia-ul-Haq.

    Zakat became compulsory during the 2nd hijrah but its complete system was imposed in the year of the 8th hijrah.

    The word Zakat occurs in the Holy Quran 32 Times

    The word Zakat is used in Makki Surahs 22 times

    Parents, Husbands, wife and children are not entitled to get Zakat.

    ND31-12-2022

    465

    Name the first Muslims who faced the first-ever ordinance of Qazf?

    Correct Answer: All of these
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Qazf in Islam is imposed on: False accusation of Zina

    Had –e- Qazaf (false Accusation) is: 80 Lashes

    Lashes means: کوڑے

    First Muslim who faced the frst ever ordinance of Qazf: Hasan ibn Sabit, Mustah ibn Asasa, Hamnah ibn Jahsh

     

    466

    قدرت کے قوانین اخلاقی قوانین سے کیسے مختلف ہوتے ہیں ان کو

    Correct Answer: توڑا نہیں جا سکتا
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    قدرت کے قوانین اخلاقی قوانین سے کیسے مختلف ہوتے ہیں ان کو توڑا نہیں جا سکتا 

    467

    ایک اچھا اخلاقی کردار کیا ہے

    Correct Answer: اچھائی کرنا درست عمل ہے
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    ایک اچھا اخلاقی کردار کیا ہے

    اچھائی کرنا درست عمل ہے

    469

    What was the origin of the Slave Dynasty Rulers?

    Correct Answer: Turks
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • Qutab ud Din Aibak was the first ruler of the Muslim dynasty in Sub-Continent
    • ......
    • Qutb al-Din Aibak  was a general of Muhammad Ghori.
    • Qutab ud Din Aibak was in charge of the Ghurid territories in northern India, and after Muhammad Ghori's death.
    • he became the ruler of an independent kingdom that evolved into the Delhi Sultanate ruled by the Mamluk dynasty.

    471

    At the order of King Bakht Nasr Hazrat Uzair (AS) spent __________ years in jail?

    Correct Answer: 70 years
    Show explanation
