    At the order of King Bakht Nasr Hazrat Uzair (AS) spent 70 years in jail.

    The Holy Quran tells the story of Prophet Uzair عَلَيْهِ ٱلسَّلَامُ as a Prophet who died for 100 years and live again




    472

    Central Asia became the part of Muslim empire during the reign of?

    Correct Answer: Waleed I
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Central Asia became the part of Muslim empire during the reign of Waleed I


    وحیدالاول خلافت امویہ کے چھٹے خلیفہ تھے۔

    ***

    Wakhan is the area that separates Pakistan from the Central Asian States

    It is a narrow strip of territory from Afghanistan to China 

    They separate Tajikistan from Pakistan and Kashmir

    It was formed in 1893 and created the Durand Line.

    It is about 350 km long

     

     

    473

    Masjid Mahabat Khan is located in _______ ?

    Correct Answer: Peshawar
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Masjid Mahabat Khan is located in:Peshawar

    Mahabat Khan masjid is a 17th-century Mughal-era mosque

    Mosque Mahabat Khan was built in 1630 and completed in 1670.

    Mahabat Khan Mosque build by Mughal emperor Shah Jahan

    Mosque name is related to Mughal Governor Peshawar “Nawab Mahabat Khan bin Ali Mardan Khan”.

    Mahabat Khan Mosque destroyed by great fire in June 1895.

    474

    حضرت ابو عبیدہ کا اصل نام کیا ہے ؟

    Correct Answer: عامر بن عبداللہ
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    ابوعبیدہ ابن الجراح جنہیں دربار رسالت سے امین الامت کا خطاب ملا قبیلہ فہر سے متعلق تھے۔ جو قریش کی ایک شاخ تھی۔ اصل نام عامر بن عبد اللہ تھا۔


    475

    When General Ayub Khan resigned from his Presidentship?

    Correct Answer: 25th March 1969
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Muhammad Ayub Khan was the second President of Pakistan from 27 October 1958 – 25 March 1969

    Ayub Khan enforced a new constitution in Pakistan on 8th June 1962

    Ayub Khan was born on 14 May 1907 in Rehana, a village in Abbottabad District 

    Ayub Khan died in Islamabad in April 1974.

    Ayub Khan's election symbol was Red Rose

    He went on to study at Aligarh Muslim University (AMU)

    Ayub Khan was an army general who seized the presidency from Iskander Mirza in a coup in 1958

    ****

    nd9-2-2023


    476

    Which country hosted the OIC foreign ministers 47th meeting in November 2021 ?

    Correct Answer: Niger
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The OIC foreign ministers' 47th meeting in November 2021in Niger

    on September 25, 1969, was OIC founded
    ***
    25 founding members of OIC at the time of its establishment.
    • OIC headquarters is located in Jadda.
    • ......
    • OIC Stands for Organization of Islamic Cooperation
    • It is the largest international organization outside of the United Nations.
    • The official languages of the OIC are Arabic, English, and French.
    • It changed its name on 28 June 2011 from the Organization of the Islamic Conference to its current name.
    • Total Current member= 57
    • The first summit of OIC held in Rabat
    • 48th Session of Foreign Ministers of OIC to be held from March 22 to 23, 2022
    • The 14th OIC meeting was held on May 31, 2019, in Makkah for Palestine.

    SH9-8-2023

    477

    Sir Syed Ahmed Khan's father name was:

    Correct Answer: Mir Muttaql
    Show explanation
    Explanation

     Mir Muttaql was the father of Sir Syed Ahmed Khan.

    Aziz-un-Nisa was the mother of sir syed Ahmed Khan's

    elder brother Syed Muhammad bin Muttaqi Khan's elder sister Safiyatun Nisa

    Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was an Islamic reformer and philosopher

    Sir Syed Ahmad Khan died on March 27, 1898.

    He is buried right along with the Sir Syed Masjid inside Aligarh University, Aligarh UP India.

    Sir Syed Ahmed Khan was born in 1817 in Delhi


    AGM 08-01-2023


    478

    "Fyuz-i-Harmain Sharifain" was written by?

    Correct Answer: Waliullah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Fyuz-i-Harmain Sharifain is one of the famous books of Shah Waliullah Dehlavi about the blessings of and spiritual revelations about the two holy cities Makkah and Madinah.

    479

    Where was the second OIC Summit Conference held?

    Correct Answer: Lahore
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The second OIC Summit Conference held from February 22, 1974 to February 24, 1974 in Lahore, Pakistan.

    The Organization was established upon a decision of the historical summit which took place in Rabat, Kingdom of Morocco on 12th Rajab 1389 Hijra (25 September 1969)

    ****

    THE 14TH ISLAMIC SUMMIT CONFERENCE Makkah, Saudi Arabia (31 May 2019)

    The 13th Islamic Summit Conference (Unity and Solidarity For Justice and Peace)  Istanbul, Republic of Turkey (14-15 April 2016)

    12th Islamic Summit Conference (The Muslim World: New Challenges & Expanding Opportunities) Cairo, Egypt (6-7 February 2013)

    11th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference (Session of The Muslim Ummah in The 21St Century) Dakar, Senegal (13-14 March 2008)

    10th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Putrajaya, Malaysia (16-17 October 2003)

    9th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Doha, State of Qatar (12-13 November 2000)

    8th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Tehran, Islamic Republic of Iran (9-11 December 1997)

    7th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco (13-15 December 1994)

    6th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Dakar, Republic of Senegal (9 - 11 December 1991)

    5th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Kuwait, State of Kuwait (26-29 January 1987)

    4th Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Casablanca, Kingdom of Morocco (16-19 January 1984)

    3rd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Makkah Al-Mukarramah, Saudi Arabia (25-28 January 1981)

    2nd Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Lahore,  Islamic Republic of Pakistan (22 - 24 February 1974)

    1st Session of The Islamic Summit Conference Rabat, Kingdom of Morocco (September 1969)

    ND04-08-2023


    480

    on agricultural lands___ is levied?

    Correct Answer: Kharaj
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Kharaj is a type of individual Islamic tax on agricultural land. Tax on agrarian land owned by non-Muslim

    The kharaj initially denoted a lump-sum duty in the 7th century.

    In the 7th century, kharaj was synonymous with jizyah, which later emerged as a per-head tax.
    SH1-8-2023

    481

    What was the meaning of Mann-o-Salwa, in Islam?

    Correct Answer: Food of Haven
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Mann-o-Salwa is a type of food that was gifted to Bani Israel (people of Prophet Musa A.S).

    There were two foods:

    • One was “Mann” and the other was “Salwa”. 
    • Mann was a kind of sweet
    • Salwa was a kind of Bird


    482

    our personal matters are controlled by?

    Correct Answer: Individual ethics
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Individual ethics refers to the ethics that a person identifies with respect to people and situations that they deal with in everyday life

     individual ethics in the organization.

    Individual ethics govern individuals' lives and acts as a foundation for understanding organizational ethics.

    Examples of Individual Ethics-

    • Honesty.
    • Loyalty.
    • Integrity.
    • Respect.
    • Selflessness.

    SH1-8-2023

    Sindh Madrasah-tul-Islam, Karachi was Granted University Status in 2012.

    486

    The holy prophet (PBUH) participated in the war of Fijar in which century?

    Correct Answer: Sixth
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Harb ibn Umayya was the father of Abu Sufyan۔

    When Prophet Muhammed ﷺ was 20 there was a battle during the Okaz fair between Quraysh (with Kanana) and Hawazin.

    This battle was called the Al-Fijar battle.

    487

    Hazrat Maryam (AS) migrated to __________ after the birth of Hazrat Essa at Bait’al’Lehm, Jerusalem?

    Correct Answer: Palestine
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Bethlehem is a Palestinian town south of  Jerusalem

    biblical birthplace of Jesus

    Isa is known as Jesus in Christianity.

    Hazrat Maryam (AS) migrated to Palestine after the birth of Hazrat Essa at Bait’al’Lehm, Jerusalem.


    Hazrat Isa (As)  was born miraculously to the most admired woman in Islam, Maryam (Mary), who was a virgin.

    The Injeel book was revealed on Hazrat Esa As

    Hazrat Isa (As) was Born Bethlehem

    Hazrat Isa (As) is known Rohullah

    488

    Hazrat Adam (AS) is a word of __________ language.

    Correct Answer: Hebrew
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    the Hebrew word for "man".

    It could be ultimately derived from Hebrew אדם ('adam) meaning "to be red",


    Adam was the     first Muslim, as the Quran, states that all the Prophets preached the same faith of Islam.

    Known for: First human being

    Spouse(s): Hawwā (حواء)

    The height of Hazrat Adam (AS) was 90 Feet

    Hazrat Adam (AS) was 130 years of age when Shees alaihissalam was born

    The language is spoken by Adam in the Garden of Eden.

    What was the Religion of Hazrat Adam?

    Islam✅

    Hazrat Adam's (AS) total age ?

    930 years✅

    Hazrat Adam (A.S) met with Holy Prophet ﷺ on the ______ heaven?

    1st✅

    How many sons did Hazrat Adam (A.S) have?

    3✅

    Where is the grave of Hazrat Adam (علیہ)?

    Sri Lanka ✅
    ND17-7-2023
    489

    Ummul Momineen ___________ migrated to Abyssinia and Madina as well.

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Ummay Habiba (RA)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    In Surah, Anfal mentions the migration of the Holy Prophet

    The first migration to Habshah (Abyssinia) took place in 613 A.D

    The relatives of Prophet Muhammad (PBUH) in the first migration to Abyssinia were Usman Ghani (R.A) and Ruqaiyah (R.A)

    There were 12 men and 4 women in the first migration to Abyssinia

    *************

    The second migration to Habshah took place in 616 A.D

    The group of emigrants' second  migrations time comprised 83 men and 18 to 19 women

    Ummul Momineen Ummay Habiba migrated to Abyssinia and Madina as well.

    490

    Which daughter of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) died the day of the victory of battle of Badr?

    Correct Answer: Ruqayyah (RA)
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Ruqayyah (RA) died on the victory day of the Battle of Badr (in Ramadan 17, 2 AH)

    She was the Second daughter of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ 

    She married (2nd time) to the third caliph Uthman

    Hazrat Usman (RA) could not participate in the Battle of Badr because his wife Ruqayyah (RA) was on his deathbed.

    She had only one son Abd Allah from Hazrat Usman (RA) but died at the age of six


    Additional Information:


    Hazrat Usman (RA) married two of the daughters of the Holy Prophet (PBUH) 

    After the death of Ruqayyah (RA), Hazrat Usman (RA) married with Umm-e-Kulthum (RA).

    Umm-e-Kulthum was the third daughter of Hazrat Muhammad 
    491

    Hazrat Khadija (RA) died on the tenth of Ramadan________ Nabwi?

    Correct Answer: 10th
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    "Hazrat Khadija's"  important Questions and information

    Hazrat Khadija (RA) was the ________ wife of Hazrat Muhammad ﷺ.

    First

    *

    Hazart Khadija (R.A) Born:    

    AD 555 Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia present-day Saudi Arabia

    *

    Hazart Khadija (R.A) Died:

    10 Ramadan BH 3  c. 619  Mecca, Hejaz, Arabia

    *

    Hazart Khadija (R.A) Resting place  

    Jannat al-Mu'alla, Mecca

    Hazrat Khadija (رضہ) passed away when she was ______ and Prophet Muhammad ﷺ was ____ years old.

    65 – 50

    At the time of the marriage, Hazrat Khadijah was 40 years old. Muhammad was 25 years.

    All of the prophet Muhammad ﷺ is children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim

    *

    Hazrat Khadija (RA) is commonly regarded by Muslims as the _______.

    Mother of the Muslims

    *

    Friend of Khadija (رضہ) ____ carried message of Nikah?

    Nafeesa

    *

    Hazrat Khadija (رضہ) belonged to the tribe of ___?

    Banu Asad

    *

    ***

    Hazrat Khadija's servant, Maysra, who accompanied the Holy Prophet on his journey was full of praise for him.

    Meysara, Lady Khadijah's (s.a) special servant.


    492

    Salat-e-Jumma was made compulsory in ____?

    Correct Answer: Madina
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    2nd Hijri Events

    ·         Regular calls for prayer.

    ·         Change direction of prayer from Jerusalem to the Kabah.

    ·         Fasting made compulsory.

    ·         Introduction of Zakat.

    ·         Hazrat Fatima Marries Hazrat Ali.

    ·         Death of Hazrat Ruqayya.

    ·         Battle of Badr took place.

    *     Salat-e-Jumma was made compulsory in Madina.

    493

    After Hazrat Umar (RA), to whom lady, script of Musaef Abi Bakr was handed over, This lady is amongst the Umhat-ul-Momenine (R.A)?

    Correct Answer: Hazrat Hafsa (R.A)
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    • After the compilation of the Quran, the copy was given to caliph, Hazrat Abu Bakr (R.A).
    • After death of Hazrat Abu Bakr, the copy transferred to the next caliph, Hazrat Umar (R.A).
    • After death of Hazrat Umar (RA), the copy was kept with Hazrat Hafza bint Umar.
    • This copy is known as Mushaf-e-Hafza
    494

    What is the real name of Marwan II?

    Correct Answer: Marwan ibn Muhammad ibn Marwan ibn al-Hakam
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The last Umayyad, Marwān II , was defeated at the Battle of the Great Zab River (750).


    495

    The Capital of Ummayad Dynesty was :

    Correct Answer: Damascus
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    The Umayyad Caliphate was the second caliphate established after the death of the Islamic prophet Muhammad.

    Capitals: Damascus, Harran

    Government: Monarchy

    Military conflict: Battle of the Zab

    The caliphate was ruled by the Umayyad dynasty, also known as the Umayyads.

    Uthman ibn Affan, the third of the Rashidun caliphs, was also a member of the clan.

    There is one sajda in surah haj.

    There are 11 Sajdah are there in Makkah Surah

    497

    Maghrib and Isha both prayers are offered togethor at:

    Correct Answer: Muzdalfa
    Show explanation
    Explanation


    • Maghrib-Isha prayers are offered together at Muzdalifa on 9th  Zill Hajj
    • ...
    • Muzdalifah was the place where Prophet Adam (AS) the Maghrib and Isha prayers together.
    • Muzdalifah is also called Al-Ibtah, meaning flat area.
    • ...
    • between 49 and 70 pebbles are collected from the ground at Muzdalifah and thrown at the three pillars at Mina, which represent the devil.
    • Muzdalifah (Arabic: مزدلفة) which is also known as al-Mashʿar or al-Mashʿar al-Ḥarām (المشعر الحرام) is a region between 'Arafat and Mina 



    498

    Tehlil means the recitation of________?

    Correct Answer: Kalma
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Tehlil" means Recitation of first Kalima



    499

    Janat Ul Muala is a graveyar in______?

    Correct Answer: Makkah
    Show explanation
    Explanation

    Jannat al-Mu'alla also known as the "Cemetery of Ma'la

    It is a cemetery to the north of Al-Masjid Al-Haram and near the Mosque of the Jinn in Makkah, Saudi Arabia.

    It is where the Islamic Prophet Muhammad's wife, grandfather, and other ancestors are buried.

    500

    How many daughters of Hazrat Muhammad PBUH?

    Correct Answer: 4
    Show explanation
    Explanation
    Prophet Muhammad ﷺ had three sons and four daughters.

    All of his children were born to Hazrat Khadijah (RA) except one son Ibrahim, who was ❤️born to Hazrat Maria al-Qibtiyah (RA).

    1. Hazrat Qasim (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ son had khadija)

    2. Hazrat Zainab (RA) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ daughter had Khadija)

    3. Hazrat Ruqayyah (RA) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ daughter had Khadija)

    4. Hazrat Umm Kulthum (RA) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ daughter had Khadija)

    5. Hazrat Fatima (RA) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ daughter had Khadija)

    6. Hazrat Abdullah (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ son had khadija)

    7. Hazrat Ibrahim (Maria al-Qibtiyah) (Prophet Muhammad ﷺ son had Hazrat Maria)

    Nice work getting this far. Consistency beats intensity — come back tomorrow and keep stacking sets.
